rulings.Case Title: JACK OLSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR MONDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 2 and 4 (strict liability) and denied as to issue 6 (punitive damages), which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. As to summary judgment, assuming without deciding that California Worker¿s Compensation law applies, there is a triable issue of material fact whether a special employment relationship existed between Mr. Olson and defendant and thus whether plaintiff¿s claims against defendant are barred by the Worker¿s Compensation Act. As to strict liability, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Olson was exposed to asbestos-containing products manufactured, distributed, sold, supplied or otherwise placed into the stream of commerce by defendant. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK OLSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR MONDAY, OCTOBER 31, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Todd Shipyards Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Olson was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant under their premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am in Dept. 503. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
